fgr — Factorio DSL compiler report

A high-level production graph compiles to a real Factorio layout; a generator-agnostic verifier traces material & fluid flow to confirm it realizes the spec; FBSR renders the result. Correctness comes from the verifier, not the picture.

6/6
basic examples
6/6
complex factories
37/37
generated stress DAGs
43/43
stress battery total

How it works

.fgr DSL ──parse──▶ Graph(spec) ──generate──▶ Layout ──verify──▶ PASS/FAIL ──▶ FBSR ──▶ PNG

Lanes: A -> B dedicated belt · A -> B, C one belt tapped by an inline inserter per consumer (no splitters) · A, B -> C multi-tap merge · A ~> B fluid lane (pipe). Machines: input/output chests, assembler, furnace, chemical plant, fluid source (pipes attach at real fluid-box tiles).

Generator: the v2 lane fabric — four deterministic passes (layer → order → place → emit), no search or rip-up, so every case compiles instantly. The verifier was hardened by an adversarial audit, so a PASS is sound.

Verifier model vs. Factorio data (FBSR)

[ok ] inserter picks at +direction, drops at -direction (all orientations) — Factorio base pickup=[0, -1], insert=[0, 1.2]
[ok ] real verify() agrees with Factorio inserter flow (probe, all dirs)
[ok ] entity footprints match the SIZE table — checked assembling-machine-2, chemical-plant, electric-energy-interface, electric-furnace, infinity-chest, infinity-pipe, inserter, loader, long-handed-inserter, pipe, pipe-to-ground, splitter, steel-chest, storage-tank, substation, transport-belt, underground-belt
[ok ] pipe-to-ground tunnels opposite its open mouth — open dir=0, underground dir=8
[ok ] chemical-plant fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((0, -1), 'input'), ((0, 3), 'output'), ((2, -1), 'input'), ((2, 3), 'output')]
[ok ] assembling-machine-2 fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((1, -1), 'input'), ((1, 3), 'output')]

Complex factories

Hand-authored multi-step builds: deep chains, reconvergence, high fan-in, furnaces, and oil/chemical fluids (pipes, tanks, mixing-free networks).

deep_linear PASS

11 nodes · 10 lanes · 89 entities · 103x5 tiles · 5 ms
# A long dependency chain (depth ~10); steel-plate is smelted in a furnace.
input iron : iron-plate
assembler a1 : iron-gear-wheel
assembler a2 : pipe
assembler a3 : engine-unit
furnace a4 : steel-plate
assembler a5 : iron-stick
assembler a6 : rail
assembler a7 : electric-furnace
assembler a8 : productivity-module
assembler a9 : production-science-pack
output out
iron -> a1 -> a2 -> a3 -> a4 -> a5 -> a6 -> a7 -> a8 -> a9 -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

flying_robot_frame PASS

14 nodes · 20 lanes · 357 entities · 86x26 tiles · 65 ms
# Deep multi-step with smelting (furnace) and fluids (pipes): flying-robot-frame.
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
fluid lube : lubricant
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
furnace steel : steel-plate
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit   # crafting-with-fluid (lubricant) -> assembler, not chemical
chemical battery : battery                  # battery is a chemistry recipe -> chemical plant
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
output frame_out
iron -> steel, gear, pipe_part, green, battery
copper -> cable, battery
cable -> green
gear -> engine
pipe_part -> engine
steel -> engine, frame
engine -> eengine
green -> eengine, frame
lube ~> eengine
acid ~> battery
eengine -> frame
battery -> frame
frame -> frame_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no undeclared fluid lanes
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

processing_unit PASS

9 nodes · 10 lanes · 155 entities · 68x10 tiles · 19 ms
# Deep electronics: copper-plate -> cable -> green -> red -> blue (processing-unit).
# Reconvergent (cable->green,red; green->red,blue). Sulfuric acid is a FLUID (~>).
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input plastic : plastic-bar
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler blue : processing-unit   # processing-unit needs sulfuric-acid (crafting-with-fluid) -> assembler
output chips
copper -> cable
cable -> green, red
iron -> green
green -> red, blue
plastic -> red
red -> blue
acid ~> blue
blue -> chips
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no undeclared fluid lanes
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

assembler cable : copper-cable

assembler greensci : logistic-science-pack

green -> arm

belt -> greensci
arm -> greensci
redsci -> red_out
greensci -> green_out

sulfuric_acid PASS

7 nodes · 6 lanes · 61 entities · 31x18 tiles · 5 ms
# Sulfuric acid: a chemical-plant takes water (FLUID, ~>) + iron + sulfur (items).
# Steel is smelted in a FURNACE. Items go to a chest, acid is piped to a tank.
input iron : iron-plate
input sulfur : sulfur
furnace steel : steel-plate
fluid water : water
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
output steel_out
output acid_tank
iron -> steel
iron -> acid
sulfur -> acid
water ~> acid
steel -> steel_out
acid ~> acid_tank

input copper : copper-plate

assembler c7 : lab
output out

cable -> green
iron -> green
green -> c0, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7
c0 -> out
c1 -> out
c2 -> out
c3 -> out
c4 -> out
c5 -> out
c6 -> out
c7 -> out

Basic examples

bus PASS

6 nodes · 5 lanes · 57 entities · 36x9 tiles · 2 ms
# One belt feeding several consumers, tapped by inline inserters (NO splitters).
#
#                         .-> assembler gears   -> out_gears
#   iron --(one belt)----+--> assembler sticks  -> out_sticks
#                         '-> output  out_raw
#
# `iron -> gears, sticks, out_raw` is a SINGLE output belt off the iron chest; each
# consumer taps it with its own inserter where the belt passes (the canonical "one
# belt feeds many machines" pattern — no splitter gadget). Contrast with three
# separate `iron -> ...` lines, which would demand three output inserters on the
# (1x1) iron chest — impossible. The shared belt needs only one.

input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler gears   : iron-gear-wheel
assembler sticks  : iron-stick
output    out_gears
output    out_sticks
output    out_raw

iron  -> gears, sticks, out_raw
gears  -> out_gears
sticks -> out_sticks
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

circuits PASS

5 nodes · 4 lanes · 54 entities · 36x6 tiles · 2 ms
# A two-stage factory with a multi-input assembler.
#
# copper-plate -> copper-cable -.
#                                +-> electronic-circuit -> output
# iron-plate -------------------'
#
# `circuit` pulls TWO lanes (iron + cable); the compiler must give it two input
# inserters on its west side and route both belt lanes without collisions. The
# iron lane also has to travel PAST the cable column to reach the circuit column.

input     copper  : copper-plate
input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler cable   : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
output    chips

copper -> cable
cable  -> circuit
iron   -> circuit
circuit -> chips
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

fanout PASS

4 nodes · 3 lanes · 27 entities · 25x6 tiles · 1 ms
# Fan-out: ONE belt off the cable assembler feeds two consumers (inline taps, no splitter).
#
#                       .-> output spool_a
#   copper -> cable ---+   (single shared belt, tapped per consumer)
#                       '-> output spool_b
#
# `cable -> spool_a, spool_b` is one output belt; the compiler runs a trunk off the
# assembler and each consumer taps it with its own inserter where the belt passes.

input     copper : copper-plate
assembler cable  : copper-cable
output    spool_a
output    spool_b

copper -> cable
cable  -> spool_a, spool_b
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

gears PASS

3 nodes · 2 lanes · 20 entities · 23x4 tiles · 1 ms
# The "hello world" factory: turn iron plates into gears, ship the gears out.
#
#   input chest --belt--> assembler (iron-gear-wheel) --belt--> output chest
#
# We declare WHAT the factory is; the compiler decides WHERE everything goes
# (assembler position, inserters, and the actual belt tile paths) and the
# verifier confirms the placed layout really carries items along each lane.

input     iron  : iron-plate
assembler gears : iron-gear-wheel
output    out

iron  -> gears
gears -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

merge PASS

4 nodes · 3 lanes · 27 entities · 25x6 tiles · 1 ms
# Merge: two sources into one machine, each tapping it directly (NO splitter).
#
#   iron_a --.
#            +--> assembler gears -> out
#   iron_b --'
#
# `iron_a, iron_b -> gears` is the mirror of fan-out: each source keeps its own
# belt and taps `gears` with its own input inserter (on a distinct face) — a
# multi-tap merge, no splitter/merge gadget.

input     iron_a : iron-plate
input     iron_b : iron-plate
assembler gears  : iron-gear-wheel
output    out

iron_a, iron_b -> gears
gears -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

science PASS

5 nodes · 4 lanes · 54 entities · 36x6 tiles · 2 ms
# A deeper, recipe-correct chain: red science (automation-science-pack).
#
#   iron-plate -> iron-gear-wheel --.
#                                    +-> automation-science-pack -> output
#   copper-plate -------------------'
#
# automation-science-pack really is 1 gear + 1 copper plate, so this is a valid
# factory. Shows a 3-deep graph and a multi-input final assembler.

input     iron    : iron-plate
input     copper  : copper-plate
assembler gears   : iron-gear-wheel
assembler science : automation-science-pack
output    out

iron   -> gears
gears  -> science
copper -> science
science -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

Stress battery — generated DAGs

37 machine-generated complex recipe graphs run through compile + verify. Every case compiles (no crashes); the remaining failures are routing through a dense field — multi-fluid oil/chem chains, very high fan-in, congested reconvergence — which need fluid-aware placement + collector merges, not the verifier. See STATUS.md.

Showcase — largest passing stress graphs

scale_6 PASS

46 nodes · 89 lanes · 3297 entities · 213x70 tiles · 14072 ms
# scale_6: kitchen-sink mega base (~46 nodes).
# Oil + electronics + mechanicals + robots + rocket parts + nuclear,
# combining deep chains, wide fan-out, heavy fan-in, and fluid branching.

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone
input uranium : uranium-ore
fluid crude : crude-oil
fluid water : water

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

chemical oil : lubricant
chemical lube : lubricant
chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
chemical battery : battery
assembler procunit : processing-unit
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit
chemical solidfuel : solid-fuel-from-light-oil
assembler rocketfuel : rocket-fuel
chemical explosives : explosives

assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler ironstick : iron-stick
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
assembler inserter : inserter
assembler belt : transport-belt
assembler rail : rail
assembler lds : low-density-structure
assembler efurnace : electric-furnace
assembler speedmod : speed-module
assembler rcu : engine-unit
assembler centrifuge_proc : electronic-circuit
assembler fuelcell : uranium-fuel-cell
assembler radar : radar
assembler logirobot : logistic-robot
assembler consrobot : construction-robot
assembler rocketpart : low-density-structure
assembler beacon : beacon

output rocket_out
output bots_out
output nuclear_out
output lube_tank

# --- fluids (pipes) ---
crude ~> oil
water ~> oil, sulfur, acid
oil ~> lube, plastic, sulfur, solidfuel, rocketfuel
lube ~> eengine, lube_tank
acid ~> battery, procunit

# --- base item distribution ---
iron -> steel, gear, green, pipe_part, ironstick, acid, battery, inserter, fuelcell, radar
copper -> cable, battery, lds
coal -> plastic, explosives
stone -> brick, rail
uranium -> centrifuge_proc

# --- intermediates ---
steel -> engine, efurnace, lds, frame, rail, beacon
brick -> efurnace
cable -> green, red, beacon
green -> red, procunit, eengine, frame, inserter, speedmod, radar, consrobot, beacon
plastic -> red
red -> procunit, speedmod, efurnace, beacon, logirobot
sulfur -> acid, explosives
gear -> engine, inserter, belt, rail, radar
ironstick -> rail
pipe_part -> engine
engine -> eengine
solidfuel -> rocketfuel
centrifuge_proc -> fuelcell

# --- top assemblies ---
procunit -> rcu
speedmod -> rcu, beacon
battery -> frame
eengine -> frame
frame -> logirobot, consrobot
lds -> rocketpart
rcu -> rocketpart
rocketfuel -> rocketpart

# --- outputs (wide fan-in) ---
rocketpart -> rocket_out
logirobot, consrobot, inserter, belt, efurnace, beacon, rail -> bots_out
fuelcell, explosives, radar -> nuclear_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no undeclared fluid lanes
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

scale_2 PASS

40 nodes · 73 lanes · 2854 entities · 185x83 tiles · 2457 ms
# scale_2: all-science megafactory (6 science packs).
# Heavy fan-in: many intermediates merge into each pack, then all packs
# merge onto one belt into the lab. Some fluids for chips/acid/lube.

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone
fluid water : water
fluid petroleum : petroleum-gas
fluid lube : lubricant

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler procunit : processing-unit
chemical battery : battery
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit

assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler ironstick : iron-stick
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler belt : transport-belt
assembler inserter : inserter
assembler firearm : firearm-magazine
assembler piercing : piercing-rounds-magazine
assembler grenade : grenade
assembler wall : stone-wall
assembler rail : rail
assembler prodmod : productivity-module
assembler efurnace : electric-furnace
assembler lds : low-density-structure
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
assembler redpack : automation-science-pack
assembler greenpack : logistic-science-pack
assembler milpack : military-science-pack
assembler bluepack : chemical-science-pack
assembler prodpack : production-science-pack
assembler utilpack : utility-science-pack

output lab

# --- fluids ---
petroleum ~> plastic, sulfur
water ~> sulfur, acid
lube ~> eengine
acid ~> battery, procunit

# --- base item distribution ---
iron -> steel, gear, ironstick, pipe_part, green, firearm, grenade, acid, battery
copper -> cable, battery, redpack
coal -> plastic, grenade
stone -> brick, rail

# --- electronics ---
cable -> green, red
green -> red, inserter, procunit, eengine, frame, prodmod
plastic -> red
red -> procunit, prodmod, bluepack
sulfur -> acid, bluepack

# --- mechanicals ---
gear -> belt, inserter, engine, redpack
ironstick -> rail
pipe_part -> engine
steel -> engine, efurnace, lds, frame, piercing, rail
brick -> efurnace, wall
engine -> eengine, bluepack
firearm -> piercing
battery -> frame
eengine -> frame

# --- sub-assemblies feeding packs ---
procunit -> utilpack
lds -> utilpack
frame -> utilpack
prodmod -> prodpack
efurnace -> prodpack
rail -> prodpack

# --- science pack fan-in (merges) ---
inserter, belt -> greenpack
piercing, grenade, wall -> milpack

# --- all packs merge onto one belt into the lab ---
redpack, greenpack, milpack, bluepack, prodpack, utilpack -> lab

scale_4 PASS

37 nodes · 72 lanes · 2740 entities · 194x72 tiles · 3448 ms
# scale_4: military / defense megafactory.
# Ammo, turrets, grenades, cannon + artillery shells. Deep shell chain
# (cannon -> explosive-cannon -> artillery) plus wide fan-in to 3 outputs.

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone
fluid water : water
fluid crude : crude-oil

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

chemical pet1 : lubricant
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical explosives : explosives
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
chemical battery : battery
chemical flammo : flamethrower-ammo

assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler firearm : firearm-magazine
assembler piercing : piercing-rounds-magazine
assembler grenade : grenade
assembler clustergrenade : cluster-grenade
assembler wall : stone-wall
assembler gate : gate
assembler gunturret : gun-turret
assembler laserturret : laser-turret
assembler rocket_item : rocket
assembler exprocket : explosive-rocket
assembler landmine : land-mine
assembler capsule : defender-capsule
assembler shell : cannon-shell
assembler exshell : explosive-cannon-shell
assembler radar : radar
assembler artyshell : artillery-shell

output ammo_out
output turret_out
output explosive_out

# --- fluids ---
crude ~> pet1, flammo
water ~> sulfur, acid, explosives
pet1 ~> sulfur, plastic
acid ~> battery

# --- base item distribution ---
iron -> steel, gear, green, firearm, grenade, acid, battery, rocket_item, radar
copper -> cable, battery, gunturret
coal -> plastic, grenade, explosives
stone -> brick

# --- intermediates ---
steel -> piercing, clustergrenade, gate, gunturret, laserturret, landmine, flammo, shell
brick -> wall
cable -> green
green -> gate, laserturret, rocket_item, capsule, radar
plastic -> shell
sulfur -> acid, explosives
gear -> capsule, radar
battery -> laserturret
firearm -> piercing
grenade -> clustergrenade

# --- explosives fan-out + shell chain ---
explosives -> clustergrenade, landmine, rocket_item, shell, exshell, artyshell, exprocket
shell -> exshell
exshell -> artyshell
rocket_item -> exprocket
radar -> artyshell

# --- outputs (fan-in) ---
firearm, piercing, flammo, capsule -> ammo_out
gunturret, laserturret, gate, wall, radar -> turret_out
grenade, clustergrenade, landmine, exprocket, artyshell -> explosive_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no undeclared fluid lanes
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

scale_5 PASS

31 nodes · 70 lanes · 2082 entities · 196x50 tiles · 4948 ms
# scale_5: production-tower stress (deep linear chains + reconvergence).
# Three deep parallel chains (assembling machines, speed modules,
# productivity modules) all reconverging on shared circuit intermediates.

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone
fluid water : water
fluid petroleum : petroleum-gas

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler procunit : processing-unit

assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler am1 : assembling-machine-1
assembler am2 : assembling-machine-2
assembler am3 : assembling-machine-3
assembler speed1 : speed-module
assembler speed2 : speed-module-2
assembler speed3 : speed-module-3
assembler prod1 : productivity-module
assembler prod2 : productivity-module-2
assembler prod3 : productivity-module-3
assembler efficiency1 : efficiency-module
assembler beacon : beacon
assembler efurnace : electric-furnace
assembler substation : substation

output machines_out
output modules_out

# --- fluids ---
petroleum ~> plastic, sulfur
water ~> sulfur, acid
acid ~> procunit

# --- base item distribution ---
iron -> steel, gear, green, am1, am2, acid
copper -> cable, substation
coal -> plastic
stone -> brick

# --- circuit core (reconvergence point) ---
steel -> am2, beacon, efurnace, substation
brick -> efurnace
cable -> green, red, beacon, substation
green -> red, am1, am2, beacon, speed1, prod1, efficiency1, substation
plastic -> red
red -> procunit, speed1, speed2, speed3, prod1, prod2, prod3, beacon, efurnace, efficiency1
sulfur -> acid
gear -> am1, am2
procunit -> speed2, speed3, prod2, prod3

# --- deep parallel chains ---
am1 -> am2
am2 -> am3
speed1 -> speed2, am3
speed2 -> speed3
prod1 -> prod2
prod2 -> prod3

# --- outputs ---
am1 -> machines_out
am2 -> machines_out
am3 -> machines_out
beacon -> machines_out
efurnace -> machines_out
substation -> machines_out
speed1 -> modules_out
speed2 -> modules_out
speed3 -> modules_out
prod1 -> modules_out
prod2 -> modules_out
prod3 -> modules_out
efficiency1 -> modules_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no undeclared fluid lanes
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

Near-misses — the dense routing tail

The largest failing stress graphs, rendered so the gap is visible: the machines and most lanes place cleanly; what's left is dense multi-fluid pipe routing and very-high-fan-in/congested item lanes (the verifier flags the exact unmet lanes).
